title: Whats next after rebuild
wikitext:
[]: What's next after rebuild?             Quote Post by trueno_twentyone » Thu Jul 23, 2009 7:33 pm It's a question that goes on in my head for quite a while. after doing rebuild of evangelion, what's gonna happen? will it be put away in the shelves of time or some other thinggy? i don't know... any ideas?

[]:             Quote Post by BattleMonkey » Thu Jul 23, 2009 7:36 pm Sequels to Rebuild of course

[]:             Quote Post by Rock-Slash » Thu Jul 23, 2009 7:41 pm Anno will make an Eva/Cthulhu mythos crossover, shamelessly similar to Children of an Elder God.

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Thu Jul 23, 2009 7:53 pm Maybe a Live Action movie series.

[]:             Quote Post by trueno_twentyone » Thu Jul 23, 2009 7:58 pm Guyver Spawn wrote: Maybe a Live Action movie series.

[]:             Quote Post by icomeanon4 » Thu Jul 23, 2009 8:19 pm Rebuild of Evangelion: Death and Rebirth. This will be a condensed version of the four movies shown as one anachronistic mess. The End of Rebuild of Evangelion. This will be an alternate version of Evangelion: Final that will feature much better animation and an assload of mindfuckery. Rebuild of Rebuild of Evangelion. This will be a series of four thirty minute episodes that will show Rebuild of Evangelion as it would have been made with better technology and a larger budget.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Thu Jul 23, 2009 8:20 pm Moving this topic to the Rebuild subforum.

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Thu Jul 23, 2009 9:12 pm More spinoffs for the sake of cash.

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Thu Jul 23, 2009 9:17 pm The Rebuild series will most likely end with 4.0 by 2013 and it seems like a Live Action movie series done by a Japanese studio like Toei would be the best choice to do next.

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Thu Jul 23, 2009 9:19 pm I'm really hoping Rebuild will be the last spin-off, at least as far as the screen is concerned.

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Thu Jul 23, 2009 9:25 pm I'd like to see Kenji Kamiyama take a stab at the franchise, actually.

[]:             Quote Post by backseatjesus » Thu Jul 23, 2009 9:26 pm Evangelion Kai

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Jul 23, 2009 9:27 pm Lucretius wrote: I'm really hoping Rebuild will be the last spin-off, at least as far as the screen is concerned.

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Thu Jul 23, 2009 9:30 pm backseatjesus wrote: Evangelion Kai

[]:             Quote Post by trueno_twentyone » Thu Jul 23, 2009 9:32 pm i was hoping that they consider doing NGE: Angelic Days into anime... a very nice spin-off of NGE.

[]:             Quote Post by backseatjesus » Thu Jul 23, 2009 9:41 pm Guyver Spawn wrote: backseatjesus wrote: Evangelion Kai

[]:             Quote Post by trueno_twentyone » Thu Jul 23, 2009 9:44 pm why not do the whole series again! it may sound funny, but if they do so, they can use the CG technology used in creating rebuild... plus they get to incorporate rebuild with the original... hehe.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Jul 23, 2009 9:49 pm Maybe after Rebuild is done, Hideaki Anno will use the profits to show the Americans how making a Live Action Anime film is done? There is still the speculative Live Action Evangelion film. Sadamoto's Evangelion manga is still ongoing. Ikari Shinji Ikusei Keikaku is still ongoing. Evangelion Gakuen Datenroku is still ongoing. More video games. Etc.

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Thu Jul 23, 2009 9:51 pm backseatjesus wrote: I actually wouldn't mind if they did it. It may be a bit of a cash in, but at least we'd get something worthwhile out of it. They don't really have to redub the Japanese portion of the series. Maybe a few fixes here or there.

[]:             Quote Post by backseatjesus » Thu Jul 23, 2009 9:57 pm Guyver Spawn wrote: backseatjesus wrote: I actually wouldn't mind if they did it. It may be a bit of a cash in, but at least we'd get something worthwhile out of it. They don't really have to redub the Japanese portion of the series. Maybe a few fixes here or there.
